result.stdout:
Usage: vt-loadtest [command]

Simple website utility.

Commands:
  save-urls    Save the sitemap as a list of URLs. Speeds up repeat runs.
  load         Run a load test on a given set of URLs. Does not spider.
  archive      Save the HTML of pages.

Options:
  -h, --help    Show help message
  --version     Show version

commands.payload:
Usage: vt-loadtest [command]

Simple website utility.

Commands:
  save-urls    Save the sitemap as a list of URLs. Speeds up repeat runs.
  load         Run a load test on a given set of URLs. Does not spider.
  archive      Save the HTML of pages.

Options:
  -h, --help    Show help message
  --version     Show version

result.stdout:
Usage: vt-loadtest archive [--path <path>] [--exclude-urls-regex <regex>...] [--output <folder path>] [--browser] [--browser-errors] [--threads <count>] [--delay] [--verbose] [--spider] [--domain <example.com>] [--domain-alts <www.example.com>...] [--cross-domain] [--ignore-content-type] [--content-search-include <main-content>] [--content-search-exclude <exclude-content>] [--content-search-terms <search term>...] [--only-save-if-term-found] [--user-agent <user-agent>] [--header <header>...] [--help]

Save the HTML of pages.

Options:
  -p, --path <path>                             URL to a sitemap or file path to a URL list. If file path ends in ".xml", file is assumed a local copy of a sitemap. (Required)
  --exclude-urls-regex <regex>...               Exclude URL paths using regular expressions.
  -o, --output <folder path>                    File path to save output to. (Required)
  -b, --browser                                 Use browser (Puppeteer) to fully render pages, including JS. Otherwise only HTML source is considered.
  -e, --browser-errors                          Log browser console errors.
  -t, --threads <count>                         Number of concurrent threads to make requests. (Default: 2)
  -d, --delay                                   Add delay between requests.
  -v, --verbose                                 Show more logging.
  --spider                                      Enable spider (find local pages linked from other pages). Limited to the primary domain or equivalent.
  --domain <example.com>                        Primary domain to archive.
  --domain-alts <www.example.com>...            Primary domain equivalents.
  --cross-domain                                Scan cross domain redirects. Note that spider link finding is still limited to only the primary domain.
  --ignore-content-type                         Ignore the Content-Type header when archiving. Otherwise only text/html is saved.
  --content-search-include <main-content>       Only search content within this CSS selector. For example, body or main. Whole document is saved by default.
  --content-search-exclude <exclude-content>    Exclude elements from the include content that match this CSS selector.
  --content-search-terms <search term>...       Search content for specific terms. Results are output to the CSV.
  --only-save-if-term-found                     Only save content if one of the search terms is found.
  --user-agent <user-agent>                     User-Agent to use for requests.
  --header <header>...                          Custom headers to include in requests. Format: "Key: Value".
  -h, --help                                    Show help message

commands.payload:
Usage: vt-loadtest archive [--path <path>] [--exclude-urls-regex <regex>...] [--output <folder path>] [--browser] [--browser-errors] [--threads <count>] [--delay] [--verbose] [--spider] [--domain <example.com>] [--domain-alts <www.example.com>...] [--cross-domain] [--ignore-content-type] [--content-search-include <main-content>] [--content-search-exclude <exclude-content>] [--content-search-terms <search term>...] [--only-save-if-term-found] [--user-agent <user-agent>] [--header <header>...] [--help]

Save the HTML of pages.

Options:
  -p, --path <path>                             URL to a sitemap or file path to a URL list. If file path ends in ".xml", file is assumed a local copy of a sitemap. (Required)
  --exclude-urls-regex <regex>...               Exclude URL paths using regular expressions.
  -o, --output <folder path>                    File path to save output to. (Required)
  -b, --browser                                 Use browser (Puppeteer) to fully render pages, including JS. Otherwise only HTML source is considered.
  -e, --browser-errors                          Log browser console errors.
  -t, --threads <count>                         Number of concurrent threads to make requests. (Default: 2)
  -d, --delay                                   Add delay between requests.
  -v, --verbose                                 Show more logging.
  --spider                                      Enable spider (find local pages linked from other pages). Limited to the primary domain or equivalent.
  --domain <example.com>                        Primary domain to archive.
  --domain-alts <www.example.com>...            Primary domain equivalents.
  --cross-domain                                Scan cross domain redirects. Note that spider link finding is still limited to only the primary domain.
  --ignore-content-type                         Ignore the Content-Type header when archiving. Otherwise only text/html is saved.
  --content-search-include <main-content>       Only search content within this CSS selector. For example, body or main. Whole document is saved by default.
  --content-search-exclude <exclude-content>    Exclude elements from the include content that match this CSS selector.
  --content-search-terms <search term>...       Search content for specific terms. Results are output to the CSV.
  --only-save-if-term-found                     Only save content if one of the search terms is found.
  --user-agent <user-agent>                     User-Agent to use for requests.
  --header <header>...                          Custom headers to include in requests. Format: "Key: Value".
  -h, --help                                    Show help message

result.stdout:
Usage: vt-loadtest load [--path <path>] [--threads <threads>] [--seconds <seconds>] [--chance-404 <chance-404>] [--delay] [--method <method>] [--verbose] [--header <header>...] [--user-agent <user-agent>] [--help]

Run a load test on a given set of URLs. Does not spider.

Options:
  -p, --path <path>                URL to a sitemap or file path to a URL list. If file path ends in ".xml", file is assumed a local copy of a sitemap. (Required)
  -t, --threads <threads>          Number of concurrent threads to make requests. (Default: 2)
  -s, --seconds <seconds>          Number of seconds to run before stopping. If zero, requests all URLs once. (Default: 5)
  -e, --chance-404 <chance-404>    Percent chance of an intentional page miss. (Default: 0)
  -d, --delay                      Add delay between requests.
  -m, --method <method>            Change the request method. (Default: GET)
  -v, --verbose                    Show more logging.
  --header <header>...             Custom headers to include in requests. Format: "Key: Value".
  --user-agent <user-agent>        User-Agent to use for requests.
  -h, --help                       Show help message

commands.payload:
Usage: vt-loadtest load [--path <path>] [--threads <threads>] [--seconds <seconds>] [--chance-404 <chance-404>] [--delay] [--method <method>] [--verbose] [--header <header>...] [--user-agent <user-agent>] [--help]

Run a load test on a given set of URLs. Does not spider.

Options:
  -p, --path <path>                URL to a sitemap or file path to a URL list. If file path ends in ".xml", file is assumed a local copy of a sitemap. (Required)
  -t, --threads <threads>          Number of concurrent threads to make requests. (Default: 2)
  -s, --seconds <seconds>          Number of seconds to run before stopping. If zero, requests all URLs once. (Default: 5)
  -e, --chance-404 <chance-404>    Percent chance of an intentional page miss. (Default: 0)
  -d, --delay                      Add delay between requests.
  -m, --method <method>            Change the request method. (Default: GET)
  -v, --verbose                    Show more logging.
  --header <header>...             Custom headers to include in requests. Format: "Key: Value".
  --user-agent <user-agent>        User-Agent to use for requests.
  -h, --help                       Show help message
